implemented_requirements.description: Add quantityLocked field to InventorySummary model — separates admin-initiated locks from checkout reservations
Add new StockMovementType enum values: LOCK, UNLOCK, RESERVATION, SALE_FINAL — enables audit trail for lock/unlock/reservation operations
Add partial index on inventory_summary for quantityLocked > 0 queries — optimizes admin lock queries filtered by store

contracts_validated: inventory_summary.quantityLocked column → INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 ✅
inventory_summary_locked_idx partial index → (storeId, quantityLocked) WHERE quantityLocked > 0 ✅
Inventory summary formula: Total = quantityAvailable + quantityReserved + quantityLocked + quantitySold ✅

rollback_strategy.note: Rollback not created as a separate 'down' migration per Prisma convention. To roll back, run: prisma migrate resolve --rolled-back 20260707100000_add_quantity_locked, then execute:
rollback_sql: -- Note: ALTER TYPE ... DROP VALUE is not supported in PostgreSQL.
-- The new enum values (LOCK, UNLOCK, RESERVATION, SALE_FINAL) will remain
-- in the StockMovementType type definition even after rollback.
-- To fully remove them, create a new type and migrate, or leave them unused.
-- They cause no harm as unused enum values.
rollback_strategy.rollback_reversibility: The quantityLocked column drop is non-destructive (existing data in other columns is preserved). Enum values cannot be removed via ALTER TYPE in PG < 14, but unused values cause no functional issues.

phase_3_handoff_notes: Backend service layer (inventory.service.ts) still references quantityReserved for admin locks — Phase 2 (or Phase 3) must update createLock() to use quantityLocked instead of quantityReserved.
The createLock() method should change from: quantityReserved: { increment: quantity } → quantityLocked: { increment: quantity }
The releaseLock() method should change from: quantityReserved: { decrement: lock.quantity } → quantityLocked: { decrement: lock.quantity }
getLowStockItems() query should SELECT quantity_locked alongside other fields.
After backend changes, frontend types (inventory.ts) and UI (InventoryView.tsx) need updating to display and manage quantityLocked.
